subject: [For Review] Sep CB Monthly Management Meeting

content.answer:
Hi Ian/Jianghong,

Please find attached deck for the upcoming Monthly CB Management Meeting on
24th Sep, Wednesday, for your review.

All sections are ready except for SCS and CNLS, which will be updated by
EOD today. @Ray Wang <ray.wang01@shopee.com> @Ning Li <ning.li@shopee.com>

Let me know for any questions.

Preparation timeline to meeting as below

Timeline

   -

   Deck Draft 2: 22 Sep (Mon)
   -

   Deck Draft 3: 23 Sep (Tues)

-- 
*Jason YU*
*Regional CB | MY & SG CB*
*Mobile*: +65 90284825
*Email*: jason.yu@shopee.com

Hi Ian/Jianghong,

Please find attached V2 of the deck for your review.

All sections are updated, except for FP&A Slide 4 and 5, which will be
added tomorrow.
I shared my feedback with SMT and SLS team offline already.

content.question: - For FBS, why are we missing targets across the board?

Slide 16

   - Why SG so bad here?

Slide 73

   - How many have we gotten for H2 so far?

Slide 88

   - Missing targets across the board other than TH?

Slide 100

   - Can see absolute numbers of previous month?

Hard to compare right now
   otherwise



Slide 106

   - How come CN SIP so bad?

content.answer:
Prioritization: new sellers with limited manpower to prepare stocks and
   shipping for different sites, tend to prioritize bigger sites, such as PH
   and MY.

Bad experience: Due to bad experiences in PH, such as PN pause and
   limited inbound quota since Aug., sellers who have not started doing SG
   tend to further postpone investing in SG.

Currently, SG has proposed a new service to do a one-time stock transfer
from 3PF to FBS, to drive up the progress. SG will also ensure of MY free
trial sellers who did not join SG free trial to join at least MY->SG Direct
or MYSG SIP

Slide 73 (now Slide 81)

How many have we gotten for H2 so far?

We have moved 51 sellers for 2025H2 so far.

For 2025H2 we aim to move 305 sellers in total, target still pending
feasibility check with CN and Local CNLS team:

   -

   CNLS mode: aim to onboard additional ~90 sellers, we will increase pitch
   success rate by facilitating vendor handshaking, FBS attack and ensuring
   regional role coverage
   -

   Direct model: we will kick off PH pitching in Oct, assume 30% success
   rate - same as other non-CNLS seller direct pitching
   -

   CB model: will kick off PH pitching in Oct, target pending CN side final
   confirmation


Slide 88 (now Slide 93)

Missing targets across the board other than TH?

For TH, the FBS MoM growth is actually lower than other markets at 13% (vs
PH MoM 23%, SG 17%). TH RR is high mainly due to revised down targets given
insufficient WH capacity and need to limit the new SKU coming in

For PH, missed targets mainly due to previous WH capacity issues (pausing
PN to stop new SKU and insufficient IB quota). While there’d be new WHs
opening in Sep and Oct, the space estimated to be still insufficient and
will need to limit new SKU coming in and revise down targets accordingly

For SG/ MY, it’s mainly due to low new seller/sku acquisition rate.
Catch-up plan: 1) to push for higher agree rate and push agreed sellers to
raise PN/IR; 2) to review top seller rejection reason 1 by 1, identify
tailored levers required for the conversion of these specific sellers, 3)
to pitch

content.question: Prioritization: new sellers with limited manpower to prepare stocks
Bad experience: Due to bad experiences in PH, such as PN pause and

content.answer:
Prioritization: new sellers with limited manpower to prepare stocks and
   shipping for different sites, tend to prioritize bigger sites, such as PH
   and MY.

Bad experience: Due to bad experiences in PH, such as PN pause and
   limited inbound quota since Aug., sellers who have not started doing SG
   tend to further postpone investing in SG.

Currently, SG has proposed a new service to do a one-time stock transfer
from 3PF to FBS, to drive up the progress. SG will also ensure of MY free
trial sellers who did not join SG free trial to join at least MY->SG Direct
or MYSG SIP

Did we find out how many say ok to MY but not to SG? When we pitch, shall
we just group both markets as 1?

There are 320 sellers (covering 630 shops, 22k ADO in MY ADO) who said okay
to MY free trial but not to SG. For these sellers, we plan to onboard them
to MYSG direct or SIP

For new sellers, we will group both market as 1 for pitching (pitch MY FBS
together with SG FBS first, and if seller still reject due to SG FBS, then
we will need sellers to minimally do SIP or MYSG Direct)


Slide 73 (now Slide 81)

How many have we gotten for H2 so far?

We have moved 51 sellers for 2025H2 so far.

For 2025H2 we aim to move 305 sellers in total, target still pending
feasibility check with CN and Local CNLS team:

   -

   CNLS mode: aim to onboard additional ~90 sellers, we will increase pitch
   success rate by facilitating vendor handshaking, FBS attack and ensuring
   regional role coverage
   -

   Direct model: we will kick off PH pitching in Oct, assume 30% success
   rate - same as other non-CNLS seller direct pitching

Which is the export market that we are pitching for?

For current batch we focus on PH, because PH has highest seller # base,
export seller # is the highest, and trending up.



   -

   CB model: will kick off PH pitching in Oct, target pending CN side final
   confirmation


Will this confuse the sellers too much? On whether they choose Direct or
Export model?

We segment pitching based on seller willingness and ops team location to
avoid confusion:

content.question: Prioritization: new sellers with limited manpower to prepare stocks
Bad experience: Due to bad experiences in PH, such as PN pause and
Seller willingness strong >> go directly with CNLS
Seller mid/low willingness + CN based >> go with CB SLS
Seller mid/low willingness + local based >> go with direct

content.answer:
High Seller COGS: RZY is a 铺货 reseller from Taobao or 1688, resulting in
   less competitive pricing. Although this seller's pricing is not ideal, they
   were able to bring in 450k SKUs which gave us the breadth of assortment
   that we wanted at the start.

Next step: We are identifying more direct TB sellers who can offer
      more competitive prices and onboard them onto TB program. (e.g 常州市希薇尔
      120 ADO selling dining tables and chairs, already onboarded to program)

Incorrect Price Setting: RZY uploads up to 30K SKUs per month to support
   assortment growth, using of blanket pricing strategies.

Next step: We are pushing RZY to improve SKU-level pricing and
      expect to correct this by 17 Oct in view of CN golden week. There has
      already been some progress here (12% of SKUs saw price reduction
after our
      NPC push)

Overall, RZY profit margin is around 10–12% of the selling price, which is
consistent with other top furniture GGPs on SHP.

Hi Ian,

Please see replies in blue below:

For current batch we focus on PH, because PH has highest seller # base,
export seller # is the highest, and trending up.

Sorry I meant import market

We will prioritize ID/MY, given highest seller interests through seller
survey and recent upward trend of sellers moving in.


Furniture and F&B still remains the issue? Do we know why?

Furniture:

   -

   82% of expensive SKUs
